question: 1. Employees whose salary is greater than JONES.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL > (SELECT SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE ENAME = 'JONES');

question: 2. Employees earning the same salary as SCOTT.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL = (SELECT SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE ENAME = 'SCOTT') AND ENAME != 'SCOTT';

question: 3. Employees hired after ALLEN.
query: SELECT ENAME, HIREDATE FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE HIREDATE > (SELECT HIREDATE FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE ENAME = 'ALLEN');

question: 4. Employees working in the same department as BLAKE.
query: SELECT ENAME, DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO = (SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE ENAME = 'BLAKE') AND ENAME != 'BLAKE';

question: 5. Employees having the same job as MILLER.
query: SELECT ENAME, JOB FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE JOB = (SELECT JOB FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE ENAME = 'MILLER') AND ENAME != 'MILLER';

question: 6. Employees earning less than the overall average salary.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL < (SELECT AVG(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP);

question: 7. Employee(s) with the minimum salary.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL = (SELECT MIN(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP);

question: 8. Employee(s) with the maximum salary.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL = (SELECT MAX(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP);

question: 9. Employees earning more than the maximum salary of CLERKs.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL > (SELECT MAX(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE JOB = 'CLERK');

question: 10. Depts with total salary > Dept 20's total salary.
query: SELECT DEPTNO, SUM(SAL) AS TOTAL_SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP GROUP BY DEPTNO HAVING SUM(SAL) > (SELECT SUM(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO = 20);

question: 11. Depts where the average salary is the highest.
query: SELECT DEPTNO, AVG(SAL) AS AVG_SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP GROUP BY DEPTNO HAVING AVG(SAL) = (SELECT MAX(AVG_SAL) FROM (SELECT AVG(SAL) AS AVG_SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP GROUP BY DEPTNO));

question: 12. Jobs having the minimum average salary.
query: SELECT JOB, AVG(SAL) AS AVG_SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP GROUP BY JOB HAVING AVG(SAL) = (SELECT MIN(AVG_SAL) FROM (SELECT AVG(SAL) AS AVG_SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP GROUP BY JOB));

question: 13. Employees earning more than the average salary of CLERKs.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL > (SELECT AVG(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE JOB = 'CLERK');

question: 14. Depts with average salary > overall average salary.
query: SELECT DEPTNO, AVG(SAL) AS AVG_SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP GROUP BY DEPTNO HAVING AVG(SAL) > (SELECT AVG(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP);

question: 15. Employees earning the second-highest salary.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL = (SELECT MAX(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL < (SELECT MAX(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP));

question: 16. Employees earning the third-highest salary.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL = (SELECT MAX(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL < (SELECT MAX(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL < (SELECT MAX(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP)));

question: 17. Employees earning more than the avg salary of Depts in DALLAS.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL > (SELECT AVG(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO = (SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT WHERE LOC = 'DALLAS'));

question: 18. Employees working in NEW YORK departments.
query: SELECT ENAME, DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO IN (SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT WHERE LOC = 'NEW YORK');

question: 19. Employees whose salary matches any salary in department 30.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL IN (SELECT SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO = 30);

question: 20. Employees earning more than all CLERKs.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL > ALL (SELECT SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE JOB = 'CLERK');

question: 21. Employees earning less than any MANAGER.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL < ANY (SELECT SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE JOB = 'MANAGER');

question: 22. Employees whose job appears in department 20.
query: SELECT ENAME, JOB FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE JOB IN (SELECT JOB FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO = 20);

question: 23. Employees earning the same salary as any CLERK.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL IN (SELECT SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE JOB = 'CLERK');

question: 24. Employees not working where any SALESMAN works.
query: SELECT ENAME, DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE JOB = 'SALESMAN');

question: 25. Employees earning more than all in department 10.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL > ALL (SELECT SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO = 10);

question: 26. Employees in departments with more than 3 employees.
query: SELECT ENAME, DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO IN (SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP GROUP BY DEPTNO HAVING COUNT(*) > 3);

question: 27. Departments that have no employees.
query: SELECT DEPTNO, DNAME, LOC FROM SCOTT.DEPT WHERE DEPTNO NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP);

question: 28. Employees who are managers (ID exists in MGR).
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE EMPNO IN (SELECT MGR FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE MGR IS NOT NULL);

question: 29. Employees who are NOT managers.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE EMPNO NOT IN (SELECT MGR FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE MGR IS NOT NULL);

question: 30. Employees working in CHICAGO departments.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO IN (SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT WHERE LOC = 'CHICAGO');

question: 31. Salary < all MANAGERS but > any CLERK.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL < ALL (SELECT SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE JOB = 'MANAGER') AND SAL > ANY (SELECT SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE JOB = 'CLERK');

question: 32. Employees whose job has more than 2 employees.
query: SELECT ENAME, JOB FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE JOB IN (SELECT JOB FROM SCOTT.EMP GROUP BY JOB HAVING COUNT(*) > 2);

question: 33. Departments where all employees get commission.
query: SELECT DEPTNO, DNAME, LOC FROM SCOTT.DEPT WHERE DEPTNO IN (SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP GROUP BY DEPTNO HAVING COUNT(*) = COUNT(COMM));

question: 34. Employees whose department has at least one SALESMAN.
query: SELECT ENAME, DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO IN (SELECT DISTINCT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE JOB = 'SALESMAN');

question: 35. More than average salary of their own department.
query: SELECT ENAME, DEPTNO, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP E1 WHERE SAL > (SELECT AVG(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP E2 WHERE E1.DEPTNO = E2.DEPTNO);

question: 36. Employees earning highest salary in each department.
query: SELECT ENAME, DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP E1 WHERE SAL = (SELECT MAX(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP E2 WHERE E1.DEPTNO = E2.DEPTNO);

question: 37. Employees earning lowest salary in each job.
query: SELECT ENAME, JOB FROM SCOTT.EMP E1 WHERE SAL = (SELECT MIN(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP E2 WHERE E1.JOB = E2.JOB);

question: 38. Salary equals the maximum salary of their specific job.
query: SELECT ENAME, JOB FROM SCOTT.EMP E1 WHERE SAL = (SELECT MAX(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP E2 WHERE E1.JOB = E2.JOB);

question: 39. Employees earning minimum salary in their department.
query: SELECT ENAME, DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP E1 WHERE SAL = (SELECT MIN(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP E2 WHERE E1.DEPTNO = E2.DEPTNO);

question: 40. Salary greater than average salary of their job.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP E1 WHERE SAL > (SELECT AVG(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP E2 WHERE E1.JOB = E2.JOB);

question: 41. Employees who are the only employee in their department.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP E1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM SCOTT.EMP E2 WHERE E1.DEPTNO = E2.DEPTNO) = 1;

question: 42. Salary greater than the salary of their manager.
query: SELECT E.ENAME, E.SAL, M.SAL AS MGR_SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP E JOIN SCOTT.EMP M ON E.MGR = M.EMPNO WHERE E.SAL > M.SAL;

question: 43. Departments that have employees (EXISTS operator).
query: SELECT DEPTNO, DNAME FROM SCOTT.DEPT D WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM SCOTT.EMP E WHERE E.DEPTNO = D.DEPTNO);

question: 44. Departments where no SALESMAN exists.
query: SELECT DEPTNO, DNAME FROM SCOTT.DEPT D WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM SCOTT.EMP E WHERE E.DEPTNO = D.DEPTNO AND E.JOB = 'SALESMAN');

question: 45. Employees whose department has no CLERKs.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP E1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM SCOTT.EMP E2 WHERE E1.DEPTNO = E2.DEPTNO AND E2.JOB = 'CLERK');

question: 46. Employees whose department has employees with commission.
query: SELECT DISTINCT E1.ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP E1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM SCOTT.EMP E2 WHERE E1.DEPTNO = E2.DEPTNO AND E2.COMM IS NOT NULL);

question: 47. Employees earning more than the median salary.
query: SELECT ENAME, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP E1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM SCOTT.EMP E2 WHERE E2.SAL > E1.SAL) < (SELECT COUNT(*)/2 FROM SCOTT.EMP);

question: 48. Employees earning more than 50% of other employees.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP E1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM SCOTT.EMP E2 WHERE E2.SAL < E1.SAL) > (SELECT COUNT(*)/2 FROM SCOTT.EMP);

question: 49. Salary equal to department-wise second-highest salary.
query: SELECT ENAME, DEPTNO, SAL FROM SCOTT.EMP E1 WHERE SAL = (SELECT MAX(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP E2 WHERE E1.DEPTNO = E2.DEPTNO AND SAL < (SELECT MAX(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP E3 WHERE E1.DEPTNO = E3.DEPTNO));

question: 50. Jobs where average salary > overall average salary.
query: SELECT DISTINCT JOB FROM SCOTT.EMP E1 WHERE (SELECT AVG(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP E2 WHERE E1.JOB = E2.JOB) > (SELECT AVG(SAL) FROM SCOTT.EMP);
